question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 2025 United Nations Military Gender Advocate of the Year Award:

1. Major Abhilasha Barak of the Indian Army was announced as the recipient of the award in May 2026.
2. She became the first-ever recipient of this award from India.
3. She was recognised for conducting gender sensitisation training for UN peacekeepers in Lebanon.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statement 2 is incorrect because Major Abhilasha Barak is the third recipient of the award from India, not the first. Statements 1 and 3 are correct. (Page 57)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: Which Regional Rural Bank (RRB) received the National Award from the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) in May 2026 for achieving 142% of its enrolment target under the Atal Pension Yojana (APY)?
rationale: Karnataka Grameena Bank (KGB) received the National Award from PFRDA for its outstanding performance, enrolling 2,49,185 APY accounts against a target of 1,75,500. (Page 57)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: In May 2026, the Asian Athletics Association (AAA) awarded India the hosting rights for two major upcoming events. Which of the following correctly matches the event with its host city?

options.A: 2027 Asian Relays - Bhubaneswar; 2028 Asian Indoor Championships - Chandigarh
options.B: 2027 Asian Relays - New Delhi; 2028 Asian Indoor Championships - Pune
options.C: 2027 Asian Relays - Chandigarh; 2028 Asian Indoor Championships - Bhubaneswar
options.D: 2027 Asian Relays - Bengaluru; 2028 Asian Indoor Championships - Mumbai
options.E: 2027 Asian Relays - Chennai; 2028 Asian Indoor Championships - Hyderabad

rationale: India was awarded the hosting rights for the 2027 Asian Relays in Chandigarh and the 2028 Asian Indoor Athletics Championships in Bhubaneswar, Odisha. (Page 58)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC) extended the deputation tenure of Anurag Garg till July 2027. He is currently serving as the Director General (DG) of which of the following organizations?
rationale: Anurag Garg's tenure as the Director General (DG) of the Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) was extended till 31 July 2027. (Page 58)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question:
Assertion (A): The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) approved the reappointment of Sandeep Bakhshi as the Managing Director (MD) & CEO of ICICI Bank in May 2026.
Reason (R): His new tenure of two years will begin on October 4, 2026, and continue till October 3, 2028.

Choose the correct option:

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true but R is false.
options.D: A is false but R is true.
options.E: Both A and R are false.

rationale: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are factually true according to the RBI approval details, but the tenure dates (Reason) are simply the details of the reappointment, not the logical explanation for why RBI approved it. (Page 59)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question:
Match the following newly appointed leaders with their respective roles/countries as of May 2026:

Column I:
1. Elias George
2. Ahmadou Al Aminou Lo
3. Janez Janša

Column II:
A. Prime Minister of Slovenia
B. Part-Time Chairman of Federal Bank
C. Prime Minister of Senegal

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Elias George was appointed Part-Time Chairman of Federal Bank. Ahmadou Al Aminou Lo was appointed PM of Senegal. Janez Janša was appointed PM of Slovenia for a fourth term. (Page 59)

question: In May 2026, the Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) increased its stake in Central Bank of India (CBI) through open market purchases. What is LIC's new total shareholding percentage in CBI following this acquisition?
rationale: LIC acquired 26.26 crore equity shares, increasing its total shareholding in the Central Bank of India from 3.16% to 6.06%. (Page 60)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: Who among the following recently assumed charge as the 48th Vice Chief of the Naval Staff (VCNS) of the Indian Navy, succeeding Vice Admiral Sanjay Vatsayan?
rationale: Vice Admiral Ajay Kochhar assumed charge as the 48th Vice Chief of the Naval Staff (VCNS), succeeding Vice Admiral Sanjay Vatsayan. (Page 60)

question: Which of the following statements is NOT correct regarding the Agni-1 missile successfully test-fired by India in May 2026?

options.A: It was test-fired from the Integrated Test Range (ITR) at Chandipur, Odisha.
options.B: It is classified as an Intercontinental Ballistic Missile (ICBM).
options.C: The test was conducted under the aegis of the Strategic Forces Command (SFC).
options.D: It is capable of carrying both conventional and nuclear warheads.
options.E: It is part of India's series of ballistic missiles developed for strategic deterrence.

rationale: Statement B is incorrect because Agni-1 is a Short-Range Ballistic Missile (SRBM), not an Intercontinental Ballistic Missile (ICBM). (Page 60)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: China recently launched the 'Shenzhou-23' spacecraft to its Tiangong Space Station. The spacecraft was carried by which of the following launch vehicles?
rationale: The Shenzhou-23 mission was launched aboard a Long March – 2F Y23 carrier rocket from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center. (Page 60)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question:
Consider the following statements about NASA's newly unveiled plan for a permanent moon base:

1. It is a USD 20 billion, 3-phased plan to establish a permanent base on the moon.
2. NASA has set the target to land astronauts on the moon as part of this plan by 2028.
3. The base will be equipped with lunar rovers and drones.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: All statements are correct. NASA unveiled a USD 20 billion 3-phased plan, aiming to set up a base in March 2027 and land astronauts in 2028, equipped with rovers and drones. (Page 60)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: An international team led by Indian scientist Dr. Ronaldo Laishram discovered a 12.6-billion-year-old cosmic structure, described as an ancient "city of galaxies." What is the name given to this structure?
rationale: The 12.6-billion-year-old cosmic structure was named the "Loktak Protocluster," after Loktak Lake in Manipur, India. (Page 60)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: India's space startup Red Balloon Aerospace Private Limited successfully launched its 1st indigenous Stratospheric Super Pressure Balloon (SPB) named 'VISTA'. The launch took place in which of the following cities?
rationale: The SPB named "VISTA" was launched at Indira Gandhi Stadium in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh. (Page 61)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: A study conducted by scientists at the ____________ revealed evidence of subsurface water ice deposits in permanently shadowed regions of lunar south polar craters, detected using India's Chandrayaan-2 radar.

options.D: Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA)

rationale: The study detailing subsurface ice detected by Chandrayaan-2 was conducted by scientists at the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), Ahmedabad. (Page 61)

question: Indian all-rounder Vijay Shankar recently announced his retirement from domestic cricket and the IPL. Which state did he represent in domestic cricket?
rationale: Vijay Shankar is an Indian all-rounder from Tamil Nadu (TN) who announced his retirement to explore overseas leagues. (Page 61)

question:
Arrange the following Formula One drivers in descending order based on their finishing positions (1st to 3rd) at the Canadian Grand Prix 2026:

1. Lewis Hamilton
2. Kimi Antonelli
3. Max Verstappen

rationale: At the Canadian GP 2026, Kimi Antonelli finished 1st, Lewis Hamilton finished 2nd, and Max Verstappen finished 3rd. Thus, the order is 2, 1, 3. (Page 61)

question: The Indian Olympic Association (IOA) announced the appointment of former Davis Cup captain Rohit Rajpal as India's Chef de Mission for the 23rd edition of the Commonwealth Games. Where will the 2026 CWG be held?
rationale: The 23rd edition of the Commonwealth Games (2026 CWG) is scheduled to be held in Glasgow, Scotland. (Page 62)

question: 19-year-old Swara Lakshmi Nair achieved the Woman International Master (WIM) title after a draw in the Ptuj International Masters tournament in Slovenia. She is the second WIM from which Indian state?
rationale: Swara Lakshmi Nair is a chess player from Bengaluru, becoming Karnataka's second WIM (after Isha Sharma). (Page 62)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding important days observed in May:

1. The World Day for Cultural Diversity for Dialogue and Development is observed annually on 21 May.
2. National Anti-Terrorism Day is observed across India on 21 May to mark the death anniversary of former PM Rajiv Gandhi.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

options.E: Only 2, but it is observed on 22 May

rationale: Both statements are correct. The UN observes Cultural Diversity Day on May 21, and India observes Anti-Terrorism Day on the same date. (Pages 63-64)

question: The UN's International Day for Biological Diversity is observed globally on May 22. What was the official theme for the year 2026?

options.A: From Agreement to Action: Build Back Biodiversity
options.D: Building a shared future for all life

rationale: The 2026 Theme for the International Day for Biological Diversity was "Acting locally for global impact." (Page 64)

question:

3. World Multiple Sclerosis Day

A. Silver ribbon

question: Which of the following days is NOT observed annually on May 24?
rationale: World Turtle Day is observed annually on May 23. Markhor Day, Schizophrenia Awareness Day, and Commonwealth Day in India are observed on May 24. (Page 64)

question:
Assertion (A): Commonwealth Day is observed annually in India on 24 May.
Reason (R): It is observed on this specific date in India to commemorate the birth anniversary of Queen Victoria in 1819.

Choose the correct option:

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true but R is false.
options.D: A is false but R is true.
options.E: Both A and R are false.

rationale: Commonwealth Day in India is observed on May 24 specifically to commemorate the birth anniversary of Queen Victoria. Globally, it is often celebrated on the second Monday of March. (Page 64)
pdf/question-bank: Current Affairs Weekly PDF - May 2026 - 4th Week

question: The International Missing Children's Day (IMCD) is annually observed across the globe on 25 May. Which specific flower symbolizes this day, representing hope for reuniting missing children with their families?
rationale: The day is symbolized by the "forget-me-not flower," representing hope for reuniting missing children with their families. (Page 65)

question: Which of the following International/World Days is NOT observed on May 28?

options.B: International Day of Action for Womens Health

rationale: World Digestive Health Day is observed on May 29. World Hunger Day, Action for Women's Health, and Menstrual Hygiene Day are all observed on May 28. (Page 65)

question: The International Day of United Nations Peacekeepers is observed annually on May 29. The year 2026 marked which anniversary of UN Peacekeeping?
rationale: 29 May 2026 marked the 78th anniversary of UN Peacekeeping, which began in 1948 with the deployment to the Middle East. (Page 65)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding Union Defence Minister Rajnath Singh's official visits in May 2026:

1. He undertook a 4-day official visit to Vietnam and South Korea.
2. In Vietnam, he held a bilateral meeting with the Deputy PM and Defence Minister Phan Van Giang.
3. In South Korea, he held bilateral talks with the Minister of National Defence Ahn Gyu-back.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: All statements are correct. Rajnath Singh visited Vietnam and South Korea from May 18 to 21, 2026, holding talks with Phan Van Giang in Hanoi and Ahn Gyu-back in Seoul. (Page 53)

question: Nikos Christodoulides undertook a 4-day official visit to India in May 2026. He is the President of which of the following countries?
rationale: Nikos Christodoulides is the President of the Republic of Cyprus. He participated in a bell-ringing ceremony at the NSE in Mumbai during his visit. (Page 54)
